description: Terms, values and formulas about the levelized cost of electricity (LCOE) from the English Wikipedia article "Levelized cost of electricity". The article is definitional, so mainly the formula relationships are modelled: the LCOE as the discounted lifetime cost per discounted electricity generation, the sum of the cost components and the ratio of LACE to LCOE. The discount rates named in the text are stored as values; a clearly marked illustrative worked example makes the three formulas verifiable via calc-validation. Source of the article: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Levelized_cost_of_electricity

words.name: cost
words.description: the amount paid or required for something.

words.name: electricity
words.description: the set of physical phenomena associated with the presence and motion of electric charge.

words.name: generation
words.description: the production of electrical energy from another source.

words.name: investment
words.description: the capital expenditure to build a generating plant.

words.name: operation
words.description: the running of a plant during its lifetime.

words.name: maintenance
words.description: the upkeep of a plant during its lifetime.

words.name: fuel
words.description: material consumed by a generator to produce energy.

words.name: lifetime
words.description: the expected operating life of a system or power station.

words.name: discount
words.description: the reduction applied to future cash flows to express them as present value.

words.name: rate
words.description: a quantity measured against another, here a proportion per period.

words.name: discounted
words.description: expressed as a net present value using the discount rate.

words.name: levelized
words.description: averaged over the lifetime of an asset as a net present value per unit of output.

words.name: avoided
words.description: of cost, the expense a generator saves the system by being available.

words.name: value
words.description: the economic worth a generator provides to the grid.

words.name: ratio
words.description: the quotient of two quantities.

words.name: threshold
words.description: a boundary value at which a decision changes; here a ratio of one.

words.name: public
words.description: of funding, financed by the state with a low social discount rate.

words.name: private
words.description: of funding, commercial for-profit financing with a higher discount rate.

words.name: lower bound estimate
words.description: the low end of an estimated range.

words.name: upper bound estimate
words.description: the high end of an estimated range.

words.name: percent
words.description: a proportion expressed as a fraction of 100.

words.name: USD
words.description: the United States dollar.

words.name: MWh
words.description: megawatt-hour, a unit of energy equal to one thousand kilowatt-hours.

words.name: times
words.description: a dimensionless multiplier or ratio.

triples.name: levelized cost of electricity
triples.description: the average net present cost of electricity generation over the lifetime of a generator: the discounted lifetime cost divided by the discounted lifetime electricity generation, i.e. LCOE = sum((I_t + M_t + F_t) / (1 + r)^t) / sum(E_t / (1 + r)^t).

triples.name: operation and maintenance cost
triples.description: the operations and maintenance expenditures over the lifetime of the generator (M_t in the LCOE formula).

triples.name: public discount rate
triples.description: the low discount rate (around 3 percent) used for public funding and social cost of capital comparisons.

triples.name: private discount rate
triples.description: the higher discount rate (about 7 to 15 percent) assumed for commercial for-profit funding.

triples.name: discounted investment cost
triples.description: the discounted lifetime sum of the investment expenditures (sum of I_t / (1 + r)^t).

triples.name: discounted operation and maintenance cost
triples.description: the discounted lifetime sum of the operations and maintenance expenditures (sum of M_t / (1 + r)^t).

triples.name: discounted fuel cost
triples.description: the discounted lifetime sum of the fuel expenditures (sum of F_t / (1 + r)^t).

triples.name: discounted lifetime cost
triples.description: the discounted lifetime total expenditure, the numerator of the LCOE; the result of the discounted lifetime cost formula.

triples.name: discounted electricity generation
triples.description: the discounted lifetime electricity generated, the denominator of the LCOE (sum of E_t / (1 + r)^t).

triples.name: levelized avoided cost of electricity
triples.description: LACE: the total discounted avoided costs divided by the total discounted lifetime output; compared with the LCOE to judge economic feasibility.

triples.name: value cost ratio
triples.description: the levelized avoided cost (value) divided by the levelized cost (cost); a project is considered economically feasible when this ratio is greater than one.
US dollars of cost or value per megawatt-hour generated.

sources.name: Wikipedia LCOE
sources.description: Wikipedia, "Levelized cost of electricity": definition, applicability, limitations and input-value selection including discount rates.

sources.name: NREL LCOE calculator
sources.description: NREL Simple Levelized Cost of Energy (LCOE) Calculator documentation, used here for the illustrative worked example.

sources.name: Lazard LCOE v14
sources.description: Lazard's Levelized Cost of Energy Version 14.0, the comparison source cited by the article.

sources.name: EIA AEO 2022 LCOE
sources.description: US EIA, Levelized Costs of New Generation Resources in the Annual Energy Outlook 2022.

sources.name: EIA LCOE LACE methodology
sources.description: US EIA methodology supplement on Levelized Cost of Electricity and Levelized Avoided Cost of Electricity (LACE).

sources.name: EIA AEO 2014 LACE
sources.description: US EIA, Levelized Cost and Levelized Avoided Cost of New Generation Resources in the Annual Energy Outlook 2014.

values.note: comparisons assuming public funding, subsidies and social cost of capital tend to choose a low discount rate of about 3 percent, favouring high-capital nuclear and renewable projects.
private investment banks tend to assume higher discount rates of 7 to 15 percent for commercial for-profit funding.
when LACE (value) exceeds LCOE (cost) the value-cost ratio is greater than 1 and the project is considered economically feasible.
illustrative worked example (not from the article): discounted lifetime investment expenditure.
illustrative worked example: discounted lifetime operations and maintenance expenditure.
illustrative worked example: discounted lifetime fuel expenditure.
illustrative worked example: the discounted lifetime total expenditure, 60000 + 30000 + 10000.
illustrative worked example: discounted lifetime electricity generated.
illustrative worked example: 100000 USD / 2000 MWh = 50 USD per MWh.
illustrative worked example: the value the generator provides to the grid, here above its LCOE so the project is feasible.
illustrative worked example: LACE 60 / LCOE 50 = 1.2, greater than 1, so feasible.

formulas.name: discounted lifetime cost formula
formulas.expression: "discounted lifetime cost" = "discounted investment cost" + "discounted operation and maintenance cost" + "discounted fuel cost"

formulas.name: levelized cost of electricity formula
formulas.expression: "levelized cost of electricity" = "discounted lifetime cost" / "discounted electricity generation"

formulas.name: value cost ratio formula
formulas.expression: "value cost ratio" = "levelized avoided cost of electricity" / "levelized cost of electricity"

calc-validation.note: illustrative: 100000 USD / 2000 MWh = 50 USD per MWh.
illustrative: LACE 60 / LCOE 50 = 1.2, greater than 1, so the project is feasible.
